title: Versa brings zero trust controls to AI agent actions
summary: Patent-pending MCP architecture verifies agent-generated actions before execution, giving operators policy control and human approval over AI-driven operations.

title: Fiber may be the AI enabler but Wi‑Fi still defines the customer experience
summary: At Fiber Connect 2026, Broadband Forum CEO Craig Thomas reality-checked the “AI is everything” thread running through the show. He told us that fiber’s value still depends on Wi‑Fi performance, service differentiation and more.

title: Opinion: Carrier 2.0: Sovereignty Without Isolation — Telecom’s New AI Balancing Act
summary: Telecom operators helped build the networks underpinning the AI economy. But what happens when the hyperscaler platforms running on top of those networks become too powerful — and too deeply embedded — to leave?

title: Nokia bets on agentic AI to move telco automation from experiment to execution
summary: Nokia’s agentic AI push focuses on fixing the operational inefficiencies in broadband and home networks that still drag on telco margins.

title: Zero trust for AI agents: SASE vendors race to secure non human users
summary: SASE platforms were built for humans. As non-human identities proliferate, vendors like Versa, Cisco and Palo Alto are furiously trying to adapt.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBirds 8 & 10 satellites prep for launch
summary: On the heels of SpaceX's IPO prospectus, which the company filed on May 20, AST SpaceMobile's Bluebird satellites 8 and 10 have just arrived in Cape Canaveral from Texas to prepare for a June 2026 launch.

title: What's up with AT&T's Build-A-Plan?
summary: AT&T this week unveiled its new Build-A-Plan wireless offer, starting at $15/month, where customers can adjust their mobile and hotspot data to fit their needs on a month-to-month basis.

title: Opinion: Telecom’s great AI dilemma: Everybody wants the future, nobody knows how to monetize it
summary: The telecom industry still has no clear answer to the problem of monetizing 5G beyond basic connectivity.

title: How will rural broadband providers survive BEAD’s next phase?
summary: Buildout is only one problem for Tier 3 broadband operators. JSI’s Adrian Fitzgerald says the real test is running efficient, scalable networks as funding shifts and competition — including satellite — heats up.

title: Memory shortage puts the squeeze on telco modernization efforts
summary: Telcos the world over are looking to modernize their IT stacks to keep up in the AI era. There’s just one big problem: a global memory shortage has completely blown up the budget.

title: What the SpaceX IPO filing says about Starlink Mobile’s wireless ambitions
summary: SpaceX’s highly anticipated S-1 filing is the latest reminder that Starlink Mobile’s ambitions go beyond filling dead zones in mostly rural areas.

title: Microsoft, Proximus team on sovereign cloud for 2 countries
summary: Microsoft and Proximus NXT are jointly developing sovereign cloud solutions that combine Azure capabilities with the local expertise of Proximus Group.

title: Neocloud Nebius inks $2.6B deal with Bloom Energy
summary: Nebius inked a deal to use Bloom Energy's fuel cell tech for its AI infrastructure buildout.

title: Opinion: Close, but no quasar
summary: The Moon may ultimately become the most important energy resource in human history.

title: Trump Mobile’s T1 phone arrives – finally
summary: It appears the Trump Mobile T1 phone is real and it’s shipping, but with a few hitches.

title: Applied Digital inks $7.5B lease for fourth AI factory campus
summary: The deal brings Applied Digital’s total contracted lease revenue to $31 billion across four AI Factory campuses.

title: Dell: Telco opportunity grows from the network edge (and AI, of course)
summary: Edge AI, managed services and automation are giving telcos a path to new revenue, says Dell's Andrew Vaz.

title: Opinion: Telcos have the AI edge – if they move now
summary: Telcos are risk averse. But they have key assets that could put them at the front of the pack as inference workloads proliferate.

title: AT&T CEO touts satellite JV, keeps AST in play
summary: AT&T CEO John Stankey gave a shoutout to AST SpaceMobile when he was asked about the new direct-to-device (D2D) joint venture that he and rivals T-Mobile and Verizon announced last week.

title: Google is building a new $5B neocloud with Blackstone – but why?
summary: Google is already one of the largest public cloud providers. So why is it working with Blackstone to build an entirely new neocloud company? We asked some analysts.

title: Is AI the 'killer app' we've all been waiting for? We asked execs at Fiber Connect
summary: The "killer app" that could push the telco industry to its limit has remained elusive until now. Is AI finally the killer app? We asked execs at Fiber Connect.

title: Michael Dell: Here's how telcos can compete in the AI era
summary: Top Dell executives tell Fierce that telcos' subscriber data, disaggregated infrastructure and sovereign cloud position are advantages in the AI economy.

title: Dell: Telco transformation is finally within reach
summary: Dell's Sandro Tavares says technology barriers to telco transformation have fallen, but changing business models is harder.

title: Lumos appoints ex-Frontier exec as its new CEO
summary: T-Mobile fiber partner Lumos snagged a key Frontier network exec to take the reins as it looks to accelerate growth.

title: M&A tracker — Great Plains scoops up Fastwyre assets
summary: It's increasingly difficult to stay on top of the ever-changing business landscape the underpins the telecom and tech industries. We're here to help!
